---
title: "simple_full_model"
author: '18004789'
date: "2025-07-18"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(rstudioapi)
library(rstan)
library(shinystan)
library(ggplot2)
library(bayesplot)
library(MASS)
library(gridExtra)
```

```{r}
X100M_male <- read_excel("100m/100m/100M_male.xls")
X100M_male$Gender <- "M"
# Add a new column 'Year' by extracting the year from 'Date'
X100M_male$Year <- as.numeric(format(X100M_male$Date, "%Y"))

sb_df <- X100M_male %>%
  group_by(original_ID, Year) %>%
  # Only keep if there are at least 3 performances in that year
  filter(n() >= 3) %>%
  slice(which.min(Result)) %>%
  ungroup()
sb_df$AgeInt <- sb_df$Year - as.numeric(format(sb_df$DOB, "%Y"))

trim_sb <- sb_df %>%
  group_by(AgeInt) %>%
  filter(AgeInt >= 17, AgeInt <= 30) %>% # now consider only ages 17 to 30
  ungroup()

# cutting down size of data
# Include athletes with at least 5 years of SBs
trim_sb <- trim_sb %>%
  group_by(original_ID) %>%
  filter(n() >= 5) %>%
  ungroup()

# Restrict to elite athletes

elite_athletes <- trim_sb %>%
  group_by(original_ID) %>%
  summarise(PB = min(Result, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  filter(PB < 10.5)

# Keep only SBs for elite athletes
trim_sb <- trim_sb %>%
  semi_join(elite_athletes, by = "original_ID")
```

```{r}
# Create a list of observed ages for each athlete
trim_observed_ages <-trim_sb %>%
  group_by(original_ID, Competitor) %>%
  summarise(M_i = list(AgeInt), count = n(), y_i = list(Result)) %>%
  ungroup()

trim_observed_ages$all_ages <- list(sort(unique(trim_sb$AgeInt)))

# getting indicies of observed ages in relation to the vector of all unique ages
trim_observed_ages$age_indices <- lapply(trim_observed_ages$M_i, function(x) {
  match(x, trim_observed_ages$all_ages[[1]])
})
```

```{r}
# Preparing data for stan model

J <- length(unique(trim_sb$AgeInt))
N <- nrow(trim_observed_ages)
# Maximum number of observations any athlete has
max_obs <- max(trim_observed_ages$count)

# Putting the vectors of ageIndicies into an Nxmax_obs matrix
age_idx <- matrix(1, nrow = N, ncol = max_obs)
for (i in 1:N) {
  inds <- trim_observed_ages$age_indices[[i]]
  len_i <- length(inds)
  age_idx[i, 1:len_i] <- inds
}

head(age_idx)

# Putting the vectors of results into an Nxmax_obs matrix
y_matrix <- matrix(0, nrow = N, ncol = max_obs)
for(i in 1:N) {
  obs_values <- trim_observed_ages$y_i[[i]]
  n_obs <- length(obs_values)
  y_matrix[i, 1:n_obs] <- obs_values
}

head(y_matrix)
```

```{r}
# Build the Stan data list:
stan_data <- list(
  N = N,                # number of athletes
  J = J,                # number of unique ages
  max_obs = max_obs,    # maximum observations per subject
  obs_count = trim_observed_ages$count,  # vector of observation counts per subject
  age_idx = age_idx,    # matrix of precomputed age indices per subject
  y = y_matrix,         # padded y matrix with athletes' observed results
  unique_ages = trim_observed_ages$all_ages[[1]],  # vector of unique ages 
  center_age = 23.5
)


simple_full <- stan_model('simple_full.stan')
```

```{r}
init_fun <- function() {
  list(
    sigma = rep(0.1, stan_data$J),
    Omega = diag(1, stan_data$J),
    beta = as.vector(fixed_model$coefficients)
  )
}

fe_fit_3000  <- sampling(simple_full, data = stan_data, init = init_fun, iter = 3000, chains = 1)

# saveRDS(fe_fit_3000, "fe_fit_3000.rds")
# simple_full_fit_reduced<- readRDS(file = 'simple_full_fit_reduced_3000.rds')
```
```{r}
fe_fit_3000<- readRDS(file = 'fe_fit_3000.rds')
```

```{r}
launch_shinystan(fe_fit_3000)
```

```{r}
unique_ages = trim_observed_ages$all_ages[[1]]
obs_count = trim_observed_ages$count

# Reduced athletes, age 17 to 30, 3000 iterations
posterior_full <- extract(fe_fit_3000)

beta_samples <- posterior_full$beta

# Posterior covariance matrix
Sigma_samples <- posterior_full$Sigma
Sigma_mean <- apply(Sigma_samples, c(2,3), mean)
print(Sigma_mean)
```
```{r}
sigma_j_samples <- posterior_full$sigma
apply(sigma_j_samples, 2, mean)           # posterior mean per age
apply(sigma_j_samples, 2, quantile, c(0.025, 0.975)) 
```



```{r}
# Posterior corr matrix
Omega_draws <- posterior_full$Omega
omega_mean <- apply(Omega_draws, c(2,3), mean)
print(omega_mean)
```


```{r}
# Residuals plot won't be good since we just consider posterior mean which is just fixed effect
c_alt <- trim_observed_ages$all_ages[[1]] - 23.5
X_poly <- cbind(
  1,
  c_alt,
  c_alt^2,
  c_alt^3,
  c_alt^4
)

summary_fit <- summary(fe_fit_3000)$summary
beta_hat <- summary_fit[grep("^beta\\[", rownames(summary_fit)), "mean"]


residuals <- list()
for (n in 1:N) {
  k <- trim_observed_ages$count[n]
  for (i in 1:k) {
    age_col <- age_idx[n, i]
    mu_hat <- as.numeric(X_poly[age_col, ] %*% beta_hat)
    residuals[[length(residuals) + 1]] <- y_matrix[n, i] - mu_hat
  }
}

# inference on residuals
residuals_vec <- unlist(residuals)
hist(residuals_vec, breaks = 30, main = "Histogram of Residuals of simpler full model", xlab = "Residual")
qqnorm(residuals_vec)
qqline(residuals_vec)
```


```{r}
set.seed(123)

n_draws <- 1500

# Posterior for the mean curve (no Sigma)
mu_draws <- matrix(NA, nrow = J, ncol = n_draws)
for (s in 1:n_draws) {
  mu_draws[, s] <- as.vector(X_poly %*% beta_samples[s, ])
}
mu_median <- apply(mu_draws, 1, median)
mu_lower  <- apply(mu_draws, 1, quantile, 0.025)
mu_upper  <- apply(mu_draws, 1, quantile, 0.975)

y_rep_draws <- matrix(NA, nrow = J, ncol = n_draws)
for (s in 1:n_draws) {
  mu_s <- as.numeric(mu_draws[, s])
  Sigma_s <- as.matrix(Sigma_samples[s, , ])
  # Draw a synthetic vector of performances at all ages for this athlete
  y_rep_draws[, s] <- MASS::mvrnorm(1, mu = mu_s, Sigma = Sigma_s)
}
y_pred_median <- apply(y_rep_draws, 1, median)
y_pred_lower  <- apply(y_rep_draws, 1, quantile, 0.025)
y_pred_upper  <- apply(y_rep_draws, 1, quantile, 0.975)

n <- 44   #240 279
ages_n <- age_idx[n, 1:obs_count[n]]
obs_perf <- y_matrix[n, 1:obs_count[n]]

plot_df <- data.frame(
  age = unique_ages,
  mu_median = mu_median,
  mu_lower = mu_lower,
  mu_upper = mu_upper,
  y_pred_median = y_pred_median,
  y_pred_lower = y_pred_lower,
  y_pred_upper = y_pred_upper
)

ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = age)) +
  # Predictive interval ribbon (widest, includes Sigma)
  # geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = y_pred_lower, ymax = y_pred_upper), fill = "orange", alpha = 0.25) +
  # Median credible interval ribbon (narrower)
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mu_lower, ymax = mu_upper), fill = "skyblue", alpha = 0.5) +
  # Median trajectory
  geom_line(aes(y = mu_median), color = "blue", size = 1) +
  # Posterior predictive median
  # geom_line(aes(y = y_pred_median), color = "orange", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
  # Observed data for this athlete
  geom_point(data = data.frame(
    age = unique_ages[ages_n],
    observed = obs_perf
  ), aes(x = age, y = observed), color = "black", size = 2) +
  labs(title = "Athlete fit", x = "Age (Years)", y = "Performance (Seconds)") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)  # 👈 centers the title
  )

```

```{r}
# n <- 6          # athlete
# k <- obs_count[n]           # number of observed ages for this athlete
# obs_idx <- age_idx[n, 1:k]  # indices of observed ages (e.g., [1, 2, 5, 7])
# y_obs <- y_matrix[n, 1:k]
# 
# S <- nrow(beta_samples)
# mu_draws <- matrix(NA, nrow = J, ncol = S)
# for (s in 1:S) {
#   mu_draws[, s] <- as.vector(X_poly %*% beta_samples[s, ])
# }
# mu_median <- apply(mu_draws, 1, median)
# mu_mean <- apply(mu_draws, 1, mean)
# 
# df_mu <- data.frame(
#   age = unique_ages,
#   mu_median = mu_median,
#   mu_mean = mu_mean
# )
# 
# df_obs <- data.frame(
#   age = unique_ages[obs_idx],
#   observed = y_obs
# )
# 
# ggplot() +
#   geom_line(data = df_mu, aes(x = age, y = mu_median), color = "blue", size = 1) +
#   #geom_line(data = df_mu, aes(x = age, y = mu_mean), color = "red", size = 1) +
#   geom_point(data = df_obs, aes(x = age, y = observed), color = "black", size = 3) +
#   labs(title = paste("Observed data for athlete", n),
#        x = "Age", y = "Performance")

plot_athlete <- function(n) {
  k <- obs_count[n]
  obs_idx <- age_idx[n, 1:k]
  y_obs <- y_matrix[n, 1:k]

  # Population mean curve
  mu_draws <- matrix(NA, nrow = J, ncol = S)
  for (s in 1:S) {
    mu_draws[, s] <- as.vector(X_poly %*% beta_samples[s, ])
  }
  mu_median <- apply(mu_draws, 1, median)
  df_mu <- data.frame(
    age = unique_ages,
    mu_median = mu_median
  )
  df_obs <- data.frame(
    age = unique_ages[obs_idx],
    observed = y_obs
  )

  ggplot() +
    geom_line(data = df_mu, aes(x = age, y = mu_median), color = "blue", size = 1) +
    geom_point(data = df_obs, aes(x = age, y = observed), color = "black", size = 1) +
    labs(x = "Age", y = "Performance")
}

n_vec <- 1:25
plot_list <- lapply(n_vec, plot_athlete)

grid.arrange(grobs = plot_list, nrow = 5, ncol = 5)

```

```{r}
# OLD
set.seed(123)
# conditional predictive distribution
# linear interpolation happens in the plot for gappy cases

n <- 171       # athlete
k <- obs_count[n]     # number of observed ages for this athlete
obs_idx <- age_idx[n, 1:k]          # indices of observed ages (e.g., [1, 2, 5, 7])
y_obs <- y_matrix[n, 1:k]           # observed performances

# The ages to predict performance for (the "future" ages)
all_age_idx <- 1:J
future_idx <- setdiff(all_age_idx, obs_idx)    # indices of unobserved ages for this athlete

S <- nrow(beta_samples)

# Matrix to store posterior predictive samples for each future age
cond_pred_draws <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(future_idx), ncol = S)

for (s in 1:S) {
  mu <- as.vector(X_poly %*% beta_samples[s, ])
  Sigma <- Sigma_samples[s, , ]
  
  # Partition means
  mu_obs <- mu[obs_idx]
  mu_pred <- mu[future_idx]
  
  # Partition covariance
  Sigma_22 <- Sigma[obs_idx, obs_idx, drop = FALSE]
  Sigma_21 <- Sigma[obs_idx, future_idx, drop = FALSE]
  Sigma_12 <- Sigma[future_idx, obs_idx, drop = FALSE]
  Sigma_11 <- Sigma[future_idx, future_idx, drop = FALSE]
  
  # Conditional mean and covariance
  mu_cond <- mu_pred + Sigma_12 %*% solve(Sigma_22, y_obs - mu_obs)
  Sigma_cond <- Sigma_11 - Sigma_12 %*% solve(Sigma_22, Sigma_21)
  
  # Sample from conditional predictive distribution
  cond_pred_draws[, s] <- as.vector(mvrnorm(1, mu_cond, Sigma_cond))
}

# Summarize posterior predictive at each future age
cond_pred_median <- apply(cond_pred_draws, 1, median)
cond_pred_lower  <- apply(cond_pred_draws, 1, quantile, 0.025)
cond_pred_upper  <- apply(cond_pred_draws, 1, quantile, 0.975)

future_ages <- unique_ages[future_idx]

df_pred <- data.frame(
  age = future_ages,
  median = cond_pred_median,
  lower = cond_pred_lower,
  upper = cond_pred_upper
)

df_obs <- data.frame(
  age = unique_ages[obs_idx],
  observed = y_obs
)

ggplot(df_pred, aes(x = age)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), fill = "blue", alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_line(aes(y = median), color = "blue", size = 1) +
  geom_point(data = df_obs, aes(x = age, y = observed), color = "black", size = 2) +
  labs(title = paste("Conditional predictive for athlete", n),
       x = "Age", y = "Performance")
```

```{r}
set.seed(123)

n <- 39
k <- obs_count[n]
obs_idx <- age_idx[n, 1:k]
y_obs   <- y_matrix[n, 1:k]

all_idx  <- 1:J
miss_idx <- setdiff(all_idx, obs_idx)
S <- nrow(beta_samples)

pred_draws_all <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = J, ncol = S)

for (s in 1:S) {
  mu    <- as.vector(X_poly %*% beta_samples[s, ])
  Sigma <- Sigma_samples[s, , ]

  mu_obs <- mu[obs_idx]
  S_oo   <- Sigma[obs_idx, obs_idx, drop = FALSE]
  r      <- y_obs - mu_obs

  Q_oo   <- solve(S_oo)
  Qr     <- Q_oo %*% r
  qdiag  <- diag(Q_oo)

  mu_loo  <- as.numeric(y_obs - Qr / qdiag)   # element-wise divide
  var_loo <- 1 / qdiag

  y_obs_pred <- rnorm(k, mean = mu_loo, sd = sqrt(pmax(var_loo, 0)))

  mu_miss <- mu[miss_idx]
  S_om    <- Sigma[obs_idx,  miss_idx, drop = FALSE]
  S_mo    <- t(S_om)
  S_mm    <- Sigma[miss_idx, miss_idx, drop = FALSE]

  alpha <- solve(S_oo, r)
  tmp   <- solve(S_oo, S_om)

  mu_cond    <- as.vector(mu_miss + S_mo %*% alpha)
  Sigma_cond <- S_mm - S_mo %*% tmp

  y_miss_pred <- as.vector(mvrnorm(1, mu_cond, Sigma_cond))
  
  draw_s <- rep(NA_real_, J)
  draw_s[obs_idx]  <- y_obs_pred
  draw_s[miss_idx] <- y_miss_pred
  pred_draws_all[, s] <- draw_s
}

# Summaries for ribbons/line
pred_median <- apply(pred_draws_all, 1, median, na.rm = TRUE)
pred_lower  <- apply(pred_draws_all, 1, quantile, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE)
pred_upper  <- apply(pred_draws_all, 1, quantile, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE)

df_pred <- data.frame(
  age    = unique_ages,
  median = pred_median,
  lower  = pred_lower,
  upper  = pred_upper
)

df_obs <- data.frame(
  age      = unique_ages[obs_idx],
  observed = y_obs
)

ggplot(df_pred, aes(x = age)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), fill = "blue", alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_line(aes(y = median), color = "blue", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(data = df_obs, aes(y = observed), color = "black", size = 2) +
  labs(title = paste("Conditional predictive for athlete", n),
       x = "Age", y = "Performance")
```

```{r}
plot_athlete <- function(n) {
  mu_draws <- matrix(NA, nrow = J, ncol = S)
  for (s in 1:S) {
    mu_draws[, s] <- as.vector(X_poly %*% beta_samples[s, ])
  }
  mu_median <- apply(mu_draws, 1, median)
  mu_lower  <- apply(mu_draws, 1, quantile, 0.025)
  mu_upper  <- apply(mu_draws, 1, quantile, 0.975)
  
  obs_idx <- age_idx[n, 1:obs_count[n]]
  y_obs <- y_matrix[n, 1:obs_count[n]]
  
  df_mu <- data.frame(
    age = unique_ages,
    mu_median = mu_median,
    mu_lower = mu_lower,
    mu_upper = mu_upper
  )
  
  df_obs <- data.frame(
    age = unique_ages[obs_idx],
    observed = y_obs
  )

  ggplot(df_mu, aes(x = age)) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mu_lower, ymax = mu_upper), fill = "skyblue", alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_line(aes(y = mu_median), color = "blue", size = 1) +
    geom_point(data = df_obs, aes(x = age, y = observed), color = "black", size = 2) +
    labs(x = "Age", y = "Performance")
}

n_vec <- c(44, 171, 190)
plot_list <- lapply(n_vec, plot_athlete)
grid.arrange(grobs = plot_list, nrow = 1, ncol = 3)
```






```{r}
# for a particular athlete, find the age at which high performance is most likely < 9.8 
# find maximum age_idx for each athlete, then only choose athletes for max_age_idx<= 8 (24 years)
age_idx_maxes <- apply(age_idx, 1, max)
athlete_indices <- which(age_idx_maxes <= 8)
```


```{r}
set.seed(123)
n <- 171
future_ages_vec <- 25:30
future_idx <- which(unique_ages %in% future_ages_vec)  # indices in unique_ages
y_thresh <- 9.9
k <- obs_count[n]     # number of observed ages for this athlete
obs_idx <- age_idx[n, 1:k]          # indices of observed ages (e.g., [1, 2, 5, 7])
y_obs <- y_matrix[n, 1:k]           # observed performances

S <- nrow(beta_samples)

# Matrix to store posterior predictive samples for each future age
future_samples <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(future_idx), ncol = S)

for (s in 1:S) {
  mu <- as.vector(X_poly %*% beta_samples[s, ])
  Sigma <- Sigma_samples[s, , ]
  
  # Partition means
  mu_obs <- mu[obs_idx]
  mu_pred <- mu[future_idx]
  
  # Partition covariance
  Sigma_22 <- Sigma[obs_idx, obs_idx, drop = FALSE]
  Sigma_21 <- Sigma[obs_idx, future_idx, drop = FALSE]
  Sigma_12 <- Sigma[future_idx, obs_idx, drop = FALSE]
  Sigma_11 <- Sigma[future_idx, future_idx, drop = FALSE]
  
  # Conditional mean and covariance
  mu_cond <- mu_pred + Sigma_12 %*% solve(Sigma_22, y_obs - mu_obs)
  Sigma_cond <- Sigma_11 - Sigma_12 %*% solve(Sigma_22, Sigma_21)
  
  y_pred <- as.vector(mvrnorm(1, mu_cond, Sigma_cond))
  
  future_samples[, s] <- y_pred
}

prob_elite <- apply(future_samples, 1, function(y) mean(y < y_thresh))


# Display results
data.frame(
  age = unique_ages[future_idx],
  prob_elite = prob_elite
)
```


```{r}
future_ages_vec <- 25:30
future_idx <- which(unique_ages %in% future_ages_vec)  # indices in unique_ages
S <- nrow(beta_samples)

y_thresh <- 9.9

prob_results <- list()

for (n in athlete_indices){
  set.seed(123)
  k <- obs_count[n]
  obs_idx <- age_idx[n, 1:k]          # indices of observed ages (e.g., [1, 2, 5, 7])
  y_obs <- y_matrix[n, 1:k]           # observed performances
  
  future_samples <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(future_idx), ncol = S)
  
  for (s in 1:S) {
    mu <- as.vector(X_poly %*% beta_samples[s, ])
    Sigma <- Sigma_samples[s, , ]
    
    # Partition means
    mu_obs <- mu[obs_idx]
    mu_pred <- mu[future_idx]
    
    # Partition covariance
    Sigma_22 <- Sigma[obs_idx, obs_idx, drop = FALSE]
    Sigma_21 <- Sigma[obs_idx, future_idx, drop = FALSE]
    Sigma_12 <- Sigma[future_idx, obs_idx, drop = FALSE]
    Sigma_11 <- Sigma[future_idx, future_idx, drop = FALSE]
    
    # Conditional mean and covariance
    mu_cond <- mu_pred + Sigma_12 %*% solve(Sigma_22, y_obs - mu_obs)
    Sigma_cond <- Sigma_11 - Sigma_12 %*% solve(Sigma_22, Sigma_21)
    
    y_pred <- as.vector(mvrnorm(1, mu_cond, Sigma_cond))
    
    future_samples[, s] <- y_pred
  }
  
  prob_elite <- apply(future_samples, 1, function(y) mean(y < y_thresh))
  prob_results[[as.character(n)]] <- data.frame(
    athlete_idx = n,
    age = unique_ages[future_idx],
    prob_elite = prob_elite
  )
  
}

elite_perf_fe_df <- do.call(rbind, prob_results)
elite_perf_fe_df$Competitor <- trim_observed_ages$Competitor[elite_perf_fe_df$athlete_idx]
```


```{r}
# verifying prediction for athletes that we have observed results for past age 25
# if we use this, there are better verifications for ages past 26

future_ages_vec <- 25:30
future_idx <- which(unique_ages %in% future_ages_vec)  # indices in unique_ages
athlete_indices_long <- which(apply(age_idx, 1, function(x) all(future_idx %in% x)))

prob_results_long <- list()
compare_results_long <- list()

y_thresh <- 9.9

for (n in athlete_indices_long){
  set.seed(123)
  # Only use data up to and including age 24 for conditioning
  valid_idx <- age_idx[n, 1:obs_count[n]]
  # number of instances that age_idx is less than 24 years 
  k <- sum(valid_idx <= 8)
  obs_idx <- age_idx[n, 1:k]
  y_obs <- y_matrix[n, 1:k]
  
  # Actual future observed results at ages 25-30:
  # Find column positions in age_idx[n, ] that match future_idx
  match_cols <- match(future_idx, age_idx[n, ])
  actual_future <- rep(NA, length(future_idx))
  actual_future[!is.na(match_cols)] <- y_matrix[n, na.omit(match_cols)]
  
  future_samples <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(future_idx), ncol = S)
  
  for (s in 1:S) {
    mu <- as.vector(X_poly %*% beta_samples[s, ])
    Sigma <- Sigma_samples[s, , ]
    mu_obs <- mu[obs_idx]
    mu_pred <- mu[future_idx]
    
    if (k >= 1) {
      Sigma_22 <- Sigma[obs_idx, obs_idx, drop = FALSE]
      Sigma_21 <- Sigma[obs_idx, future_idx, drop = FALSE]
      Sigma_12 <- Sigma[future_idx, obs_idx, drop = FALSE]
      Sigma_11 <- Sigma[future_idx, future_idx, drop = FALSE]
      
      mu_cond <- mu_pred + Sigma_12 %*% solve(Sigma_22, y_obs - mu_obs)
      Sigma_cond <- Sigma_11 - Sigma_12 %*% solve(Sigma_22, Sigma_21)
      #Sigma_cond <- Sigma_cond + diag(1e-8, nrow(Sigma_cond))
    } else {
      # no data ≤ 24 → unconditional predictive
      mu_cond <- mu_pred
      Sigma_cond <- Sigma[future_idx, future_idx, drop = FALSE]
    }
      
    y_pred <- as.vector(mvrnorm(1, mu_cond, Sigma_cond))
    
    future_samples[, s] <- y_pred
  }
  
  prob_elite <- apply(future_samples, 1, function(y) mean(y < y_thresh))
  
  # Add actual observed
  compare_results_long[[as.character(n)]] <- data.frame(
    athlete_idx = n,
    age = unique_ages[future_idx],
    prob_elite = prob_elite,
    actual_result = actual_future,
    elite_achieved = actual_future < y_thresh
  )
}

elite_compare_fe_df <- do.call(rbind, compare_results_long)
elite_compare_fe_df$Competitor <- trim_observed_ages$Competitor[elite_compare_fe_df$athlete_idx]
```



```{r}
# Out of current 23 yr olds, whose gonna be the fastest at age a = 28

# choose athletes for max_age_idx== 7 (23 years), (not necessarily 21 today since season's best might not have been recorded- but leave for later)
athlete_indicies_23 <- which(age_idx_maxes == 7)

print(athlete_indicies_23)

trim_observed_ages$Competitor[athlete_indicies_23]
```

```{r}
set.seed(123)
# commpare 2 athletes: 190, 304
a <- which(unique_ages == 27)
pair_ids <- c(304, 390)
pair_pred <- matrix(NA, nrow = 2, ncol = S)

for (i in seq_along(pair_ids)) {
  
  n <- pair_ids[i]
  k <- obs_count[n]
  obs_idx <- age_idx[n, 1:k]
  y_obs <- y_matrix[n, 1:k]
  
  for (s in 1:S) {
    mu <- as.vector(X_poly %*% beta_samples[s, ])
    Sigma <- Sigma_samples[s, , ]
    mu_obs <- mu[obs_idx]
    mu_a <- mu[a]
    Sigma_22 <- Sigma[obs_idx, obs_idx, drop = FALSE]
    Sigma_21 <- Sigma[obs_idx, a, drop = FALSE]
    Sigma_12 <- Sigma[a, obs_idx, drop = FALSE]
    Sigma_11 <- Sigma[a, a, drop = FALSE]
    
    # singular age being predicted, so univariate case
    mu_cond <- as.numeric(mu_a + Sigma_12 %*% solve(Sigma_22, y_obs - mu_obs))
    sigma2_cond <- as.numeric(Sigma_11 - Sigma_12 %*% solve(Sigma_22, Sigma_21))
    pair_pred[i, s] <- rnorm(1, mu_cond, sqrt(sigma2_cond))
  }
}

pair_win_counts <- pair_pred[1, ] < pair_pred[2, ]
mean(pair_win_counts)
```

```{r}
set.seed(123)
a <- which(unique_ages == 28)

y_future_23 <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(athlete_indicies_23), ncol = S)

for (ii in seq_along(athlete_indicies_23)) {
  n <- athlete_indicies_23[ii]
  k <- obs_count[n]
  obs_idx <- age_idx[n, 1:k]
  y_obs <- y_matrix[n, 1:k]
  
  for (s in 1:S) {
    mu <- as.vector(X_poly %*% beta_samples[s, ])
    Sigma <- Sigma_samples[s, , ]
    mu_obs <- mu[obs_idx]
    mu_a <- mu[a]
    Sigma_22 <- Sigma[obs_idx, obs_idx, drop = FALSE]
    Sigma_21 <- Sigma[obs_idx, a, drop = FALSE]
    Sigma_12 <- Sigma[a, obs_idx, drop = FALSE]
    Sigma_11 <- Sigma[a, a, drop = FALSE]
    
    # singular age being predicted, so univariate case
    mu_cond <- as.numeric(mu_a + Sigma_12 %*% solve(Sigma_22, y_obs - mu_obs))
    sigma2_cond <- as.numeric(Sigma_11 - Sigma_12 %*% solve(Sigma_22, Sigma_21))
    y_future_23[ii, s] <- rnorm(1, mu_cond, sqrt(sigma2_cond))
  }
}

# Get predicted minimum value and index for each sample
fastest_info <- apply(y_future_23, 2, function(v) {
  ii <- which.min(v)
  c(athlete = athlete_indicies_23[ii], value = v[ii])
})

fastest_info <- t(fastest_info)
colnames(fastest_info) <- c("athlete_idx", "value")
```


```{r}
winners <- as.integer(fastest_info[, "athlete_idx"])
prob_best_23_28 <- sapply(athlete_indicies_23, function(id) mean(winners == id, na.rm = TRUE))

results_23_28 <- data.frame(
  athlete_idx = athlete_indicies_23,
  prob_best = prob_best_23_28
)

results_23_28$Competitor <- trim_observed_ages$Competitor[results_23_28$athlete_idx]
```


```{r}
# Out of current athletes, who are running, who is projected to be in 2022 world champs final (1 yr ahead of dataset)

current_athlete_ids <- trim_sb %>%
  filter((2022 - as.numeric(format(DOB, "%Y"))) <= 30) %>%
  distinct(original_ID) %>%
  pull(original_ID)

current_athlete_indicies <- which(trim_observed_ages$original_ID %in% current_athlete_ids)
```

```{r}
set.seed(123)
y_world_final <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(current_athlete_indicies), ncol = S)

for (ii in seq_along(current_athlete_indicies)) {
  n <- current_athlete_indicies[ii]
  k <- obs_count[n]
  obs_idx <- age_idx[n, 1:k]
  y_obs <- y_matrix[n, 1:k]
  
  # current age + 1yr 
  a_plus_1 <- age_idx[n, k] + 1
  
  for (s in 1:S) {
    mu <- as.vector(X_poly %*% beta_samples[s, ])
    Sigma <- Sigma_samples[s, , ]
    
    mu_obs <- mu[obs_idx]
    mu_a1 <- mu[a_plus_1]
    
    Sigma_22 <- Sigma[obs_idx, obs_idx, drop = FALSE]
    Sigma_21 <- Sigma[obs_idx, a_plus_1, drop = FALSE]
    Sigma_12 <- Sigma[a_plus_1, obs_idx, drop = FALSE]
    Sigma_11 <- Sigma[a_plus_1, a_plus_1, drop = FALSE]
    
    # singular age being predicted, so univariate case
    mu_cond <- as.numeric(mu_a1 + Sigma_12 %*% solve(Sigma_22, y_obs - mu_obs))
    sigma2_cond <- as.numeric(Sigma_11 - Sigma_12 %*% solve(Sigma_22, Sigma_21))
    
    y_world_final[ii, s] <-  rnorm(1, mu_cond, sqrt(sigma2_cond))
  }
}

# For each sample (column), rank the times, rankings[ii, s]: athlete ii's rank (1 = fastest) in sample s
# when rankings are tied, randomly allocate 
rankings <- apply(y_world_final, 2, rank, ties.method = "random")

# For each athlete, calculate the proportion of samples where their rank is ≤ 8:
pr_top8 <- rowMeans(rankings <= 8, na.rm = TRUE)

fe_results_world_final <- data.frame(
  athlete_idx = current_athlete_indicies,
  pr_top8 = pr_top8
)

fe_results_world_final$Competitor <- trim_observed_ages$Competitor[fe_results_world_final$athlete_idx]
```
